directions.name: Restore the new best custom branch `5dd9f0d` and discard the grouped-CTA traversal
directions.hypothesis: Round 5 cleanly falsified the L2-launch-order clue as an implementation branch. The grouped traversal kept correctness, slightly improved the hot-band kernel to about 40.70 us, and even nudged headline tensor activity up, but overall runtime regressed from 29.432832 ms to 30.692336 ms. That means the remap is not a viable surface for the next 25 rounds even if one sub-kernel metric looks nicer. The first move should therefore be to restore the correctness-valid best custom branch `5dd9f0d` before trying another family.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Not a new bottleneck attack; this is a branch reset after a structurally negative CTA-traversal experiment.
directions.risk: Low. The restore is local and measured. The only cost is one recovery round, but continuing from the slower grouped-traversal branch is less defensible.
directions.stop_condition: If the restored branch does not come back near the recorded 29.43 ms behavior, stop and investigate implementation drift before another experiment.

directions.name: Human idea bank conflict: test a `Right Left Right Left` warp-local B consumer sweep on the restored best branch
directions.hypothesis: After the restore, the next most aligned user-provided direction is still warp-local bank/consumer tuning with no extra shared memory and no CTA repack. The current best branch already proved that B-consumer ordering matters, but it only tried one edge-in mirrored sweep. A second warp-local order that groups columns in a `Right Left Right Left` style may change bank behavior or operand delivery enough to reduce the remaining short-scoreboard / feed tax without reopening shared-memory footprint problems.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Residual B-side shared-to-register delivery and bank behavior inside the 64x64 hot-band micro-tile.
directions.risk: Moderate to high. This stays within the no-extra-shared rule, but the current B path is already fairly tuned, so the wrong sweep can easily regress.
directions.stop_condition: Reject if the alternate sweep raises `mio_throttle`, raises registers, or slows the hot-band kernel.

directions.name: Human idea coalescing + async copy: retune hot-band cp.async ownership after the restore
directions.hypothesis: If the next gain is still on the staging side rather than on block order, the remaining low-level experiment is to keep the restored best branch intact and adjust `cp.async` ownership so each warp copies more contiguous stripes of A or B into shared. That attacks instruction regularity and LSU behavior without changing tile shape or stage count.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Global-to-shared staging instruction overhead and copy ownership regularity.
directions.risk: Moderate. The code motion is contained, but the copy mapping can easily regress if it disturbs the current steady-state barrier rhythm.
directions.stop_condition: Reject if barrier stall or hot-band time gets worse.

notes: Round 6/30 starts from a negative but informative experiment. The grouped CTA traversal that was meant to hint better L2 locality preserved correctness and slightly improved the isolated hot-band kernel time, but it regressed end-to-end runtime by about 1.26 ms, so it is not a viable surface for continued optimization. Recommended direction dir_01 therefore restores the newly established best custom branch `5dd9f0d` before another family is tried. Dir_02 and dir_03 both stay aligned with the user-provided human ideas on top of that restored branch: first a warp-local bank/conflict consumer sweep that still respects the no-extra-shared rule, then a cp.async ownership retune if the consumer-side variant does not pay off.
